response:
the hexagonal center possesses an area calculated by splitting the shape into six equilateral triangles where each side measures thirty meters yielding a total surface of approximately one thousand four hundred fifty square meters. this geometric result derives from the standard formula involving the square of the side length multiplied by three times the square root of three divided by two.

the circular garden inscribed within the hexagon has a radius equal to the apothem of the shape which measures fifteen times the square root of three meters. calculating the area of this central circle using the standard formula yields a value of six hundred seventy-five times pi square meters.
